approvedApproach: The dog does not send an opening message after selection. Only the avatar and text entry field appear.
reason: Dogs wait for a human command. The forced first input creates the reveal and trains the visitor how the experience works.

approvedApproach: Allow visitors to roll the dice and receive one of the four dogs.
reason: Supports discovery and makes all four first interactions necessary even while the Collie is the full MVP.

approvedApproach: Build the complete conversational journey for the Collie first.
reason: It reduces complexity while proving the central system, data model and transfer mechanics.

approvedApproach: Write enough opening behaviour for Labrador, Terrier and Boxer to support random selection and transfers.
reason: The first response is the key joke and must work for every dog.

approvedApproach: Use approved site data, FAQs and curated knowledge records rather than unrestricted live web research.
reason: Improves safety, speed, accuracy, maintenance and character consistency.

approvedApproach: During pre-launch, the Collie offers the 30% mailing-list code rather than Buy Now.
reason: The Collie is strategic and organised. Direct sales enthusiasm belongs mainly to the Labrador.

approvedApproach: Allow repeated transfers as part of the joke.
reason: Visitors gradually realise that every operator is another dog.

approvedApproach: Use an approximate hidden ceiling of 20 human submissions.
reason: Prevents accidental endless sessions without visibly breaking the joke.

approvedApproach: The Boxer can pretend to transfer the visitor and accidentally close the chat.
reason: Provides a deliberate comic circuit breaker and satisfying ending.

approvedApproach: Mini-game actions do not consume conversation turns.
reason: A game would otherwise use the interaction allowance before it can be completed.

approvedApproach: Start with Nine-Square Sheep Management, Missing Sheep and Kennel Sketch Recognition.
reason: They are short, family-safe, inexpensive and can run inside the text area.

approvedApproach: Collie target is 500 canonical records: 100 each in mathematics, science and nature, geography, English literature and British history.
reason: This creates a surprisingly capable but bounded character. Alternative phrasings multiply coverage.

approvedApproach: Exclude dedicated time, calendar and grammar/language question sets.
reason: They do not fit the intended canine worldview or current creative focus.

approvedApproach: The experience is written and operated in English only.
reason: Keeps the MVP maintainable and avoids false claims of multilingual canine fluency.

approvedApproach: Use occasional Geordie phrasing but avoid dense phonetic spelling.
reason: The humour depends on intelligence and precision, not a regional caricature.

approvedApproach: Store scientific facts accurately, then add clearly comic canine interpretations.
reason: Allows educational value without flattening the brand's humour.

approvedApproach: Build static first. Add canine behaviour animation as a later reusable state library.
reason: Prevents animation from delaying the conversational proof of concept.

approvedApproach: Create a science-led article about head tilting and the future possibility of canine communication literacy.
reason: Supports the intelligence essay and provides content for future animation and chatbot jokes.
